summary: Gruber analyzes an Apple company-wide memo about leaking that was itself leaked to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. The memo details Apple's increasingly aggressive anti-leak efforts, including firing employees and pursuing criminal prosecution. Gruber notes the deep irony that nearly every specific leak example cited in the memo appears to have been leaked to the same reporter who obtained the memo itself. He highlights that Apple caught and fired the person who leaked the iOS 11 gold master (which revealed the iPhone X name) and multiple employees feeding information to 9to5Mac. The most striking revelation is that Apple had 12 of 29 caught leakers arrested in 2017, marking a significant escalation beyond mere termination.
key_points: Apple's internal memo on leaking was itself leaked to the very reporter whose sources it describes catching and firing
Nearly every specific leak example in the memo traces back to Mark Gurman, creating layers of irony
The iOS 11 GM leak three days before the event revealed the iPhone X name, which had been the one major unknown despite a year of other rumors
Apple reported catching 29 leakers in 2017 with 12 arrested, escalating consequences from termination to criminal prosecution
Apple's digital forensics capabilities have advanced to the point where leakers are being caught faster than ever
money_quotes: It seems possible that every single specific example cited by Apple in this memo was someone leaking to Mark Gurman.
Makes you wonder who had the balls to send this memo to him.
We'll be getting into Inception territory if the leaker of the memo on leakers getting fired for leaking to Gurman gets fired for leaking it to Gurman.
Getting fired for leaking — we all knew that happened. But this is the first I've heard of leakers being prosecuted criminally and going to jail.
Apple is not fucking around regarding leaks.
Gurman doesn't mention that the meeting was leaked to Gurman himself — the person who leaked this story was caught and fired.
I can see why Gurman and Bloomberg might not want to emphasize that.
key_insight: The supreme irony of Apple's anti-leak memo being leaked to the very reporter whose sources it describes catching reveals both the futility and the escalating seriousness of Apple's war on leaks.
